coin_profile.name: Venice Token
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.
coin_profile.description: Venice Token (VVV) — a Cash-Flow Protocol ranked #85 by market cap.

VVV.name: Venice Token

dilution_control.label: Dilution Control
dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

moat.rationale: VVV shows weak competitive defensibility with low protocol maturity (4/10) and dilution control (4/10), making it easily forked without significant switching costs. While network demand scores reasonably (8/10), this is undermined by poor capital efficiency (5/10) and holder value accrual (5/10), indicating the protocol captures limited economic value for token holders despite user growth. With only 57.95% circulating supply and subpar fundamentals across most dimensions, VVV lacks meaningful moats comparable to established L1s or even DeFi primitives, positioning it as a cash-flow protocol with high replication risk and limited defensibility.

chart_zones.mos_note: Price above speculative fair value — no margin of safety.

2026.net_issuance_note: Current snapshot only. Historical supply data requires on-chain indexing not available via free APIs.

meta.data_note: No protocol fee or TVL data found for this token on DeFiLlama. Charts 1 and 2 are unavailable. Charts 3 and 4 show current-year supply and dilution data from CoinGecko.

forecast_meta.growth_basis_note: Annual growth rates derived from quality score 5.0/10 and survivability 43.3%. Base: 7.0% p.a., Bull: 13.0% p.a., Bear: -26.9% p.a.
forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.
forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (7.0%/yr): projected growth based on this token's quality score (5.0/10). Bull (13.0%/yr): adoption accelerates and network usage grows strongly. Bear (-26.9%/yr): adoption slows or competition erodes the token's position. The bear scenario is steeper than usual because survivability is only 43.3% — this token has weaker dilution control or holder value accrual, making it more vulnerable in a downturn.
forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, and Holder Revenue are unavailable: this token has no DeFiLlama fee tracking. Valuation relies entirely on network activity and market positioning signals. Network Activity (NVT) treats 24h trading volume as a proxy for on-chain utility — it fires when volume is available and the resulting IV falls within 0.1x–10x of current price. Metcalfe Network Value (MC/Volume ratio) confirmed the NVT signal and contributed to the median pool. Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment penalises the large gap between circulating and fully-diluted supply — future token unlocks are expected to create selling pressure. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.
forecast_meta.price_history_note: Limited price history available (1 year(s)). This token may be recently listed or have insufficient on-chain data.

VVV.iv_method: Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, Dilution Adjustment

watchlist_signal.reason: Weak fundamentals or heavily overextended price relative to value — higher-quality opportunities available elsewhere.

bull_points: Down 20% in the past week — near-term selling pressure may be creating a short-term buying opportunity
Any positive news catalyst — exchange listing, partnership, or protocol upgrade — could trigger a sharp near-term rally from current levels
Crypto market sentiment is cyclical — Venice Token's short-term setup may improve as macro uncertainty fades
bear_points: Up 77% over 30 days — strong recent momentum increases the probability of a short-term mean reversion
Regulatory developments in major markets could create short-term selling pressure
Without a near-term catalyst, Venice Token may continue to consolidate or drift lower

bull_points: Long-term adoption thesis for Cash-Flow Protocol assets remains structurally intact
As the crypto ecosystem matures, Venice Token's established position should compound in value
Continued development activity and ecosystem growth support a constructive long-term view
bear_points: No multi-year fee data exists for Venice Token — the protocol's long-term economic durability remains unproven
Technology or regulatory disruption risk cannot be ruled out over a 3-5 year horizon
Long-term execution risk remains a key variable for any crypto investment

metric_assessments.fee_growth: The score of 6 reflects moderate fee generation relative to market cap, but without concrete annual fee data, there's uncertainty about whether fee growth is actually accelerating or plateauing. Venice appears to be capturing some value from its users, but the lack of transparent fee metrics makes it impossible to confirm if this revenue stream is expanding or contracting over time. This opacity is the primary constraint—without visibility into trending fee data, confidence in sustainable revenue growth remains limited.
metric_assessments.capital_efficiency: A score of 5 indicates Venice deploys capital at only an average level compared to peers in its ranking tier, suggesting its revenue-generating activities aren't notably more productive than competing protocols. The protocol is functioning as intended operationally, but it hasn't demonstrated the ability to extract outsized returns from its deployed assets. Better-positioned protocols in this category generate meaningfully higher returns per dollar of capital at work, which Venice has not yet achieved.
metric_assessments.holder_value_accrual: The score of 5 signals that token holders capture only a middling share of the protocol's economic value, with no clear or attractive mechanism routing profits back to VVV holders. While the protocol generates some cash flows, the distribution mechanism or buyback structure appears weak or inefficient. Unless holder value capture improves materially—through stronger tokenomics, governance rewards, or direct buybacks—token upside will remain capped relative to protocol cash generation.
metric_assessments.network_demand: The strong score of 8 is backed by solid volume-to-market-cap data of 10.22%, indicating healthy trading activity and genuine user engagement at this valuation level. This is one of Venice's genuine strengths—there's active usage and transaction flow supporting the protocol. However, network demand alone doesn't guarantee profitability or sustainable token value if fee capture and holder rewards remain weak, as they do here.
metric_assessments.dilution_control: The low score of 4 reflects a concerning 57.95% circulating supply ratio, meaning nearly 58% of all tokens are already in circulation with 42% still to unlock. Combined with an FDV-to-MC ratio of 1.73, significant dilution headwinds remain as tokens gradually enter the market. This supply overhang creates persistent downward pressure on price per token, even if network metrics improve, making long-term token appreciation structurally challenging.
metric_assessments.protocol_maturity: Ranked #85 in market cap yet classified as a cash-flow protocol, Venice sits outside the proven top tier where sustainability is typically validated. The score of 4 reflects the reality that at this rank, the protocol lacks the history, proven business model longevity, or market confidence of leaders like Uniswap or Curve. For a cash-flow protocol to justify investment, maturity and track record matter heavily—Venice's mid-tier status means fewer guarantees about survival or continued relevance during downturns.

quality_narrative.classification_reason: Venice Token's 'Dangerous' rating stems from a cluster of critical weaknesses masking behind one genuine strength: while network demand is solid (8/10), the protocol scores poorly on dilution control (4), holder value capture (5), capital efficiency (5), and remains unproven in maturity (4). The combination of heavy token dilution ahead (42% unlocked), middling fee capture, and lack of clear paths to investor returns creates uncompensated downside risk. Without improvements in tokenomics and value distribution to holders, the protocol's strong usage metrics offer little protection against long-term value destruction.
quality_narrative.investment_narrative: Venice Token is a cash-flow generating protocol with legitimately engaged users (evidenced by 10%+ volume-to-cap ratio), but it suffers from broken tokenomics that prevent holders from capturing proportional economic upside. The protocol's revenue mechanisms exist, but neither generate enough fee growth relative to market cap nor distribute those rewards effectively to token holders, leaving VVV holders in a position where they absorb all downside risk from the 42% pending token dilution without meaningful profit participation. A 57-day 77% price surge creates dangerous momentum-chasing conditions, and the lack of transparency around fee trends makes fundamental valuation impossible. This is a protocol investors should monitor for structural improvements in holder value accrual and supply discipline, but current tokenomics make it unsuitable for most portfolios—the risk-reward is inverted.
quality_narrative.quality_conclusion: Venice Token scores 5.0/10 (Dangerous) as a Cash-Flow Protocol. The overall quality profile reflects the protocol's maturity, fee economics, and token supply dynamics relative to peers.

coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. coin_profile.description: VeChain (VET) — a Cash-Flow Protocol ranked #104 by market cap.

dilution_control.label: Dilution Control
dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

moat.rationale: VET has modest moat strength primarily from enterprise adoption in supply chain (notably Walmart China) and established governance infrastructure, but faces significant structural weaknesses: low fee growth (4), weak holder value accrual (4), and immature protocol fundamentals (2) indicate limited economic defensibility despite excellent dilution control (10). The protocol is easily forked with no technical barriers, lacks meaningful network effects beyond specific enterprise integrations, and offers minimal token utility—holders capture little real economic value while users can switch to competing supply chain solutions at low cost, positioning VET closer to a service platform than a defensible protocol.

forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.
forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (3.0%/yr): projected growth based on this token's quality score (4.7/10). Bull (9.0%/yr): adoption accelerates and network usage grows strongly. Bear (-29.0%/yr): adoption slows or competition erodes the token's position. The bear scenario is steeper than usual because survivability is only 53.3% — this token has weaker dilution control or holder value accrual, making it more vulnerable in a downturn.
forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, and Holder Revenue are unavailable: DeFiLlama has no fee data for this token. This typically means the protocol does not generate trackable on-chain fees, or its fee reporting is not yet indexed. Network Activity (NVT) treats 24h trading volume as a proxy for on-chain utility — it fires when volume is available and the resulting IV falls within 0.1x–10x of current price. Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment did not fire: FDV/MC is within the acceptable range for this quality tier, so no dilution penalty was applied. Only 2 methods contributed to the median. More data sources (DeFiLlama fee history) would improve confidence in this estimate. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.

watchlist_signal.reason: Weak fundamentals or heavily overextended price relative to value — higher-quality opportunities available elsewhere.

bull_points: Fear & Greed score of 23 (Fear) signals extreme pessimism — historically a contrarian entry signal for VeChain over the next 1-3 months
Any positive news catalyst — exchange listing, partnership, or protocol upgrade — could trigger a sharp near-term rally from current levels
Crypto market sentiment is cyclical — VeChain's short-term setup may improve as macro uncertainty fades
bear_points: Broader crypto market volatility and macro headwinds present near-term downside risk
Regulatory developments in major markets could create short-term selling pressure
Without a near-term catalyst, VeChain may continue to consolidate or drift lower

bull_points: Multi-year on-chain fee data confirms the protocol has generated real revenue across both bull and bear market cycles
Over 99% of the maximum supply is already in circulation — future dilution risk is minimal for long-term holders
Continued development activity and ecosystem growth support a constructive long-term view
bear_points: Competitive intensity in the Cash-Flow Protocol category may erode VeChain's position over time
Technology or regulatory disruption risk cannot be ruled out over a 3-5 year horizon
Long-term execution risk remains a key variable for any crypto investment

metric_assessments.fee_growth: VeChain scores 4/10 on fee growth, reflecting limited visibility into how protocol revenues are expanding relative to market valuation. The lack of disclosed annual fee data means we cannot assess whether transaction volumes are translating into meaningful fee capture for token holders. This opacity is the primary constraint—without clear fee metrics, investors cannot verify that network adoption is actually benefiting VET holders financially.
metric_assessments.capital_efficiency: VeChain ranks 5/10 for capital efficiency, sitting at position #104 in market cap with a quality percentile of 47.1, suggesting moderate efficiency relative to peers in its category. The protocol is generating some value from its deployed capital, but it is not outperforming competing projects on a risk-adjusted basis. The middling rank indicates capital is being deployed, but returns to shareholders remain unproven at scale.
metric_assessments.holder_value_accrual: VeChain scores 4/10 on holder value accrual, indicating limited mechanisms for token holders to capture protocol upside through staking or validator rewards. While staking exists as a value route, the modest score signals that accrual mechanics are either underdeveloped, diluted across too many participants, or simply not competitive with alternative yield sources. This directly limits why someone would hold VET long-term versus trading it.
metric_assessments.network_demand: VeChain shows 3.04% volume-to-market-cap ratio, earning a 4/10 score that reflects modest but real transactional activity relative to its $500M market valuation. The protocol is seeing genuine usage, which validates its enterprise supply-chain positioning, but the ratio remains thin—suggesting either limited daily transaction volume or that the network has not achieved network effects at scale. This trading activity is a real positive signal, but it needs to grow materially to support a higher valuation.
metric_assessments.dilution_control: VeChain scores a perfect 10/10 on dilution control, with 99.16% of tokens already in circulation and an FDV-to-MC ratio of just 1.01, indicating virtually no future supply overhang. This is a rare structural advantage—token holders face almost no risk of value erosion from new issuance, and the protocol has already completed its distribution phase. This is one of the few genuinely strong points in VeChain's favor and removes a major risk vector.
metric_assessments.protocol_maturity: VeChain scores only 2/10 on protocol maturity despite being ranked #104 globally, indicating it lags significantly behind more established competitors in development depth, ecosystem adoption, or demonstrated economic moats. This gap—a top-104 project with bottom-tier maturity scoring—suggests VeChain remains unproven relative to older, more entrenched protocols at similar or lower market caps. The low score reflects market skepticism about whether its supply-chain positioning translates into durable competitive advantages.

quality_narrative.classification_reason: VeChain is rated Dangerous because its quality score of 4.7/10 and 47.1th percentile ranking signal a protocol that is struggling to justify its market valuation. The combination of weak fee growth visibility, unproven holder value accrual, low protocol maturity, and only moderate network demand creates an unfavorable risk-reward profile—token holders lack clear mechanisms to capture upside, while the project has not yet demonstrated the staying power of top-tier competitors.
quality_narrative.investment_narrative: VeChain is an enterprise-focused blockchain protocol targeting supply-chain transparency and asset authentication, positioned in a real-world use case with genuine demand signals showing 3% volume-to-market-cap activity. The protocol's primary strength is its nearly complete token circulation (99.16%) and zero future dilution risk, which eliminates a major shareholder concern. However, the core weakness is opacity around fee capture and lack of clear token holder economics—despite enterprise adoption, it is unclear how much value actually accrues to VET holders, and the protocol ranks poorly on maturity compared to competitors at similar market caps. The 30-day price decline of 18% and middling capital efficiency scores suggest the market has already priced in skepticism about execution. For beginners, VeChain represents a speculative bet on enterprise blockchain adoption with structural constraints on token value capture, making it suitable only for risk-tolerant investors willing to wait for proof that supply-chain volumes translate into holder returns.
quality_narrative.quality_conclusion: VeChain scores 4.7/10 (Dangerous) as a Cash-Flow Protocol. The overall quality profile reflects the protocol's maturity, fee economics, and token supply dynamics relative to peers.

coin_profile.description: Virtuals Protocol (VIRTUAL) — a Cash-Flow Protocol ranked #111 by market cap.

VIRTUAL.name: Virtuals Protocol

dilution_control.label: Dilution Control
dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

moat.rationale: VIRTUAL exhibits minimal protocol moat despite strong network demand (10/10) due to critical weaknesses in competitive defensibility, token utility, and ecosystem lock-in. The protocol is easily forkleable with no inherent technical barriers, token holders capture minimal cash flows (holder_value_accrual: 5), and low protocol maturity (2/10) suggests unproven long-term viability. Without established network effects that create switching costs or genuine economic value accrual to token holders, VIRTUAL remains vulnerable to competition and replacement.

watchlist_signal.reason: Weak fundamentals or heavily overextended price relative to value — higher-quality opportunities available elsewhere.

bull_points: <cite index="2-5">VIRTUAL rose 5% to $0.74 in May 2026 amid renewed interest in the AI-agent crypto sector</cite>, and <cite index="2-22">ACP Node v2 release on May 4, 2026 represents a major infrastructure upgrade for the AI agent network</cite>, creating catalysts for near-term momentum over 3-6 months.

<cite index="2-7">Coinbase listing coincided with a BBB rating and positive outlook from TokenInsight as of April 28, 2026</cite>, providing institutional legitimacy that could attract new buyers in the current fear-driven market (current Fear & Greed Score: 34).

<cite index="2-1">$0.70 has been a strong resistance while $0.65 is serving as a support</cite>, and with the current price at $0.72, a breakout above resistance could trigger a sharp recovery given the 1.53 FDV-to-MC ratio and -6.7% 7-day decline creating technical setup for mean reversion.
bear_points: <cite index="6-8">Trading volume of VIRTUAL is down 32.80% in the last 24 hours, signalling a recent fall in market activity</cite>, suggesting weakening near-term momentum and potential consolidation or drift lower without a catalyst.

<cite index="2-13">Analysts note a clear bearish bias with price below key moving averages and weak volume</cite>, compounded by <cite index="3-15">the token entering a 'slow dump' trendline, bleeding out over several months on fading volume</cite>.

<cite index="2-18">The token is trapped in a narrow range with a break above $0.70 needed for a bullish shift, while a drop below $0.65 could renew selling</cite>, and broader macro headwinds or regulatory developments could push VIRTUAL toward its support zone in the next 3-6 months.

bull_points: <cite index="6-4">In 2025, the protocol announced major partnerships including with Animoca Brands to integrate AI agents into its gaming ecosystem, and a collaboration with Zyfai and BasisOS to launch an 'Agentic Fund of Funds'</cite>, demonstrating ecosystem expansion that supports long-term adoption and value accrual over 1-5 years.

<cite index="6-10,6-11,6-12">As of 2025, the protocol expanded to become a multi-chain platform with deployments on Ethereum mainnet, Solana, and Ronin, with tokenized AI agents able to autonomously engage in commerce with humans or other agents on-chain, each paired with VIRTUAL in locked liquidity pools enabling co-ownership and revenue generation</cite>.

<cite index="6-14,6-15,6-16">VIRTUAL stakers participate in protocol treasury decisions through veVIRTUAL (launched July 2025) and on-chain DAO votes, may receive airdrops of other agent tokens, and earn from AI agent services as users pay inference costs in VIRTUAL with revenues flowing through the buyback mechanism</cite>, creating multiple revenue streams and holder value accrual.
bear_points: The network economics data shows no multi-year fee history (annual_fees_usd: null) and no established P/S ratio, meaning the protocol's long-term cash-flow durability and unit economics remain unproven relative to established DeFi protocols over a 1-5 year horizon.

<cite index="10-31">There is a high degree of uncertainty over whether Virtuals Protocol can maintain its position at the front of the AI agent token sector</cite>, exposing investors to competitive displacement risk as rival AI protocols with stronger funding or infrastructure emerge.

With a circulating supply of only 65.7% and FDV-to-MC ratio of 1.53, significant dilution risk exists post-unlock, combined with the "Dangerous" quality classification (score: 5.0), indicating structural tokenomics risks and execution uncertainty that could compress valuations over the long term.

metric_assessments.fee_growth: The score of 6 reflects moderate fee-generation capacity relative to market cap, but without disclosed annual fee data, this assessment relies on volume-to-market-cap proxies that may not capture the full picture of actual protocol revenue. Virtuals appears to be extracting value from user activity, which is necessary for any cash-flow protocol, but the inability to verify real earnings makes it hard to trust whether fees are actually growing or just moving with volatility. A higher score would require transparent, audited fee disclosures that prove sustainable monetization beyond trading volume spikes.
metric_assessments.capital_efficiency: The score of 5 places Virtuals in the middle of its peer set by rank proxy, suggesting it converts deployed capital into value at an average rate compared to other protocols at similar market-cap levels. The protocol hasn't demonstrated outsized returns on capital invested by users or token holders—it's neither wasting resources nor optimizing them exceptionally well. Without evidence of compounding returns or improving unit economics, there's little reason to expect efficiency to accelerate from here.
metric_assessments.holder_value_accrual: The score of 5 indicates that token holders are capturing value at a baseline rate, but the lack of transparent fee-sharing mechanisms or buyback programs limits how much wealth actually concentrates in their hands. Most value created by the protocol may be flowing to users, developers, or reinvestment rather than directly to VIRTUAL holders. Until the protocol implements clear, verifiable cash-flow distribution to token holders, this metric will remain stuck at middling levels.
metric_assessments.network_demand: The score of 10 is the protocol's standout strength, with network demand at 23.7% of market cap—a notably high ratio showing robust user activity and transaction volume relative to its valuation. This demonstrates real adoption and ecosystem engagement, which are the foundation any young protocol needs to survive. However, high volume-to-market-cap ratios can also indicate speculative trading rather than organic utility, so demand must translate into sustained fees and genuine user retention to justify the current valuation.
metric_assessments.dilution_control: The score of 4 reflects a concerning situation: 65.7% of tokens are already in circulation, and the FDV-to-MC ratio of 1.53 means another 53% dilution is still pending. This creates persistent selling pressure as unvested tokens enter the market, eroding the purchasing power of current holders. The protocol has limited runway before fully diluted supply is reached, and without a clear plan to burn or lock tokens, new supply will continue to overhang the price.
metric_assessments.protocol_maturity: The score of 2 is the most telling weakness—Virtuals ranks only #111 by market cap, placing it in the early, unproven stage of protocol development. At this rank, the protocol has not yet demonstrated the staying power, feature completeness, or regulatory clarity that comes with maturity. Most protocols at this level fail to survive the next market cycle, so even strong metrics in other areas cannot offset the fundamental risk of being an immature project without a track record.

quality_narrative.classification_reason: Virtuals Protocol earned a Dangerous rating because it combines early-stage immaturity (rank #111, score 2) with heavy future dilution (65.7% circulating, 1.53x FDV-to-MC) and unverified fee mechanisms, despite showing bright spots in network demand. The protocol is monetizing activity well, but without audited revenue, a clear path to protocol stability, or token holder protections, the upside is heavily offset by execution and supply risk. A young protocol with speculative demand is inherently exposed to trend shifts that could evaporate its user base and revenue overnight.
quality_narrative.investment_narrative: Virtuals Protocol is an early-stage AI-agent or autonomous-system platform riding strong user demand (23.7% volume-to-market-cap ratio), but it remains fundamentally unproven at rank #111 with only a 5/10 quality score. The protocol's core strength lies in its ability to attract activity and generate fees from that engagement, yet no audited annual fee data exists to verify that revenue is real or sustainable. The critical risk is token supply: 35% dilution still pending means continuous selling pressure will weigh on the token price regardless of business performance, and at this early stage, user demand could evaporate if a competitor emerges or market sentiment shifts. This is a high-risk, high-conviction bet suited only for investors comfortable with near-total-loss scenarios in exchange for exposure to a potential breakout protocol—not a cash-flow play yet, but a leveraged bet on adoption that may or may not materialize.
quality_narrative.quality_conclusion: Virtuals Protocol scores 5.0/10 (Dangerous) as a Cash-Flow Protocol. The overall quality profile reflects the protocol's maturity, fee economics, and token supply dynamics relative to peers.

forecast_meta.growth_basis_note: Annual growth rates derived from quality score 4.1/10 and survivability 46.7%. Base: 3.0% p.a., Bull: 9.0% p.a., Bear: -37.0% p.a.
forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.
forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (3.0%/yr): projected growth based on this token's quality score (4.1/10). Bull (9.0%/yr): adoption accelerates and network usage grows strongly. Bear (-37.0%/yr): adoption slows or competition erodes the token's position. The bear scenario is steeper than usual because survivability is only 46.7% — this token has weaker dilution control or holder value accrual, making it more vulnerable in a downturn.
forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, and Holder Revenue are unavailable: this token has no DeFiLlama fee tracking. Valuation relies entirely on network activity and market positioning signals. Network Activity (NVT) treats 24h trading volume as a proxy for on-chain utility — it fires when volume is available and the resulting IV falls within 0.1x–10x of current price. Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment did not fire: FDV/MC is within the acceptable range for this quality tier, so no dilution penalty was applied. Only 2 methods contributed to the median. More data sources (DeFiLlama fee history) would improve confidence in this estimate. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.
forecast_meta.price_history_note: Limited price history available (1 year(s)). This token may be recently listed or have insufficient on-chain data.

metric_assessments.fee_growth: Vision scores 4/10 here, indicating modest fee generation relative to its market cap size. The protocol is collecting revenue, but the volume-to-market-cap proxy suggests fees aren't scaling impressively—there's activity happening, but not enough to justify stronger confidence in revenue durability. What's working is that fees exist and the protocol has proven it can extract economic value from users; what's missing is evidence of accelerating fee capture or expanding transaction volume that would push this higher.
metric_assessments.capital_efficiency: The 5/10 rank-proxy score reflects that Vision sits at #236 by market cap—a mid-tier position that's neither proving exceptional capital deployment nor failing outright. The protocol has attracted meaningful capital and deployed it into operations, suggesting basic competence in allocating resources. However, being ranked 236th means it hasn't achieved the efficiency gains or competitive advantages that would let it outperform peers with similar or smaller capital bases.
metric_assessments.holder_value_accrual: Vision receives a 5/10 for estimated value accrual, meaning token holders capture some economic benefit from protocol activity—likely through fee distribution or buyback mechanisms—but the benefit isn't compelling relative to the risk profile. The protocol does appear to funnel returns to holders rather than hoarding fees, which is positive; the limiting factor is that those returns remain modest and haven't created a meaningful wealth compounding effect for long-term holders.
metric_assessments.network_demand: At 2.56% volume-to-market-cap ratio, Vision scores 4/10, showing genuine user activity and transaction throughput that validates baseline demand. The protocol is being used meaningfully enough that it generates real fees and consistent volume, which rules out complete abandonment or lack of utility. The constraint is that this 2.56% ratio is moderate—not weak enough to signal dead protocol, but not strong enough to suggest network effects or viral adoption are kicking in.
metric_assessments.dilution_control: Vision's 8/10 score here is its strongest metric, with 88.03% of tokens already in circulation and only a 1.14x FDV-to-MC ratio. This means most of the token supply is already distributed, limiting the risk of sudden inflation from future emissions that would crush holder returns. The upside is clear structural protection against dilution; the minor constraint is that 12% of tokens remain to be released, and how those are distributed will matter for sentiment and price stability.
metric_assessments.protocol_maturity: The 1/10 score reflects Vision's #236 ranking, placing it outside the top tier of established protocols with proven multi-year track records and hardened infrastructure. This isn't about fundamental failure—it means Vision lacks the brand recognition, ecosystem depth, and battle-tested history that early-stage investors typically bet on at higher valuations. The protocol is real and operational, but it hasn't yet demonstrated the staying power or competitive moat that would justify confidence in long-term dominance.

quality_narrative.classification_reason: Vision is rated Dangerous because it combines a weak overall quality score of 4.1/10 (41st percentile) with critical immaturity at rank #236 and an exceptionally poor protocol_maturity score of 1/10. While dilution control is strong and network demand exists, the lack of proven longevity, modest fee economics, and mid-tier capital efficiency create a risk profile where token value depends heavily on speculative adoption rather than defensible fundamentals. The 25% price decline over 30 days reflects market skepticism that matches this risk assessment.
quality_narrative.investment_narrative: Vision is a cash-flow protocol positioned at mid-tier scale (#236), generating real fees and serving an active user base, but it hasn't yet proven the competitive advantages or ecosystem stickiness that justify higher valuations. The protocol's main strength is tight token supply discipline—88% circulating with minimal future dilution—which protects against death spirals; its fundamental weakness is immaturity and lack of proof that its economics will compound over time. Network demand exists at 2.56% volume-to-MC, but this is moderate and doesn't suggest network effects are dominant. For risk-tolerant investors betting on adoption acceleration, Vision offers exposure to a real, fee-generating protocol with controlled dilution; for conservative investors, the combination of maturity risk, modest unit economics, and recent price decline makes this a speculative position better suited to small allocation sizes or wait-and-see approaches until the protocol demonstrates multi-year stability and growing user lock-in.
quality_narrative.quality_conclusion: Vision scores 4.1/10 (Dangerous) as a Cash-Flow Protocol. The overall quality profile reflects the protocol's maturity, fee economics, and token supply dynamics relative to peers.
